source.title: On the computational architecture of the neocortex. II. The role of cortico-cortical loops
source.note: Biological Cybernetics 66(3):241-251. This is the direct conceptual predecessor to Rao & Ballard (1999). Part I (1991) established thalamo-cortical loops; Part II establishes the cortico-cortical prediction/residual framework. Often cited as the origin of the typed-prediction-downward / prediction-error-upward architecture in predictive coding.

confidence_rationale: This paper establishes the typed-prediction-descent principle as a theoretical proposition about neocortical architecture — the direct conceptual ancestor of the Schwiedrzik & Freiwald (2017) result and the framework that Rao & Ballard (1999) formalised computationally. Mumford explicitly proposes that top-down (feedback) cortico-cortical connections carry predictions/interpretations generated by the higher area, while bottom-up (feedforward) connections carry residuals — the part of lower-level activity not explained by the higher-level prediction. Because predictions are generated by the higher-level model, they inherit the representational format of that level by construction: typed prediction descent is a theorem of this architecture, not an empirical observation. This is the foundational theoretical prior art for the inference Schwiedrzik & Freiwald's result is used to license. Confidence is high because the mapping to ARC-022's E1->E2 typed-object interface is direct and the argument is domain-independent by construction. Penalised modestly because the paper is theoretical/mathematical rather than empirically supported at the time of writing (no electrophysiological confirmation existed in 1992), and because Mumford's formulation is for a two-level exchange rather than the five-level pipeline ARC-022 specifies.
failure_signatures: Mumford's framework is a general-purpose prediction/residual architecture and does not specify the representational content of predictions at each level. ARC-022's specific claim that z_gamma transitions (rather than sensory predictions) are the typed objects at the E1->E2 interface is not derivable from Mumford alone.

The original paper discusses cortico-cortical loops in a two-area framework. The extension to a five-stage pipeline (E1->E2->DMN->Goal/Avoid->Hippocampus) requires iterating the framework across heterogeneous computation types, which Mumford does not explicitly address.

The 1992 paper pre-dates the experimental validation tradition. The claim that this specific framework characterises neocortex was not empirically confirmed until Rao & Ballard (1999) and Bastos et al. (2012).

mapping.source_claim_statement: Top-down cortico-cortical feedback connections carry the higher area's prediction (interpretation) of activity in the lower area. Bottom-up feedforward connections carry the residual — the activity in the lower area not accounted for by the higher area's prediction. Because predictions are generated by the higher-level model, they carry the representational format (abstraction level, typed properties) of the higher area, not the lower area.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-022 claims that each layer in the hierarchical pipeline operates on typed output objects from the layer below — specifically, that E2 operates on z_gamma transition objects generated by E1, rather than re-processing raw sensory streams. Mumford's framework provides the foundational theoretical argument for why this must be so: in any system where higher-level models generate predictions for lower levels, those predictions are typed at the higher level by construction. The E1->E2 interface is a direct instantiation of Mumford's cortico-cortical loop: E2 generates predictions about E1's latent dynamics, E1 computes residuals relative to those predictions, and the typed-object character of what flows between them is a consequence of the prediction-generation architecture. This establishes the typed-object pipeline principle as theoretically necessary rather than empirically contingent.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Mumford's paper addresses a general cortico-cortical exchange architecture, not specifically the five-level pipeline of ARC-022 or the specific typed objects (z_gamma, trajectory bundles, Goal/Avoid valuations) that ARC-022 assigns to each interface. The theoretical grounding is for the principle; the specific instantiation in ARC-022 remains a design hypothesis requiring empirical confirmation at each stage. Mumford also wrote before the laminar specificity evidence (Bastos et al. 2012) that anatomically instantiated the prediction-downward/error-upward dichotomy.
mapping.source_context: Pure theory paper by David Mumford (then at Harvard, later Brown), written from a mathematician's perspective on cortical architecture. Part of a two-paper series. The paper proposed the predictive coding framework seven years before Rao & Ballard (1999) formalised it computationally, and 25 years before Schwiedrzik & Freiwald (2017) confirmed it electrophysiologically in the face-processing hierarchy.

confidence_components.notes: Biological Cybernetics is a high-quality venue for computational neuroscience theory; Mumford is a Fields Medal mathematician whose theoretical contributions are widely considered rigorous. Source quality is high. Mapping fidelity is high because the prediction/residual exchange is exactly the mechanism ARC-022's typed-object pipeline relies on. Transfer risk is low because the argument is domain-independent by construction — it applies to any hierarchical system where higher levels generate predictions for lower levels, not just the visual hierarchy. The main risk is the step from two-level exchange to five-level pipeline, which is iterative rather than directly demonstrated.
